heroHeadline: CFS Becomes First Fusion Company to Apply to US Grid as NIF Advances Laser Upgrade
heroSubtitle: Commonwealth Fusion Systems applied to PJM Interconnection on April 28 — the first-ever fusion grid interconnection application in the US — as SPARC reaches ~75% completion targeting first plasma in 2027. Days earlier, NIF's Enhanced Fusion Yield Capability laser upgrade (2.2→2.6 MJ) cleared Critical Decision-1, while MIT Technology Review's cost analysis placed fusion's learning rate at 2–8%, ahead of fission but slower than renewables.
footerNote: Data sourced from DOE/LLNL/NNSA, ITER Organization, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, ARPA-E, UK Government, peer-reviewed journals (Nature, Physical Review Letters), and the Fusion Industry Association. All milestones independently verified.
